free_text: Fine structure of analogue states in 51Mn, 53Mn and
55Mn
The TUNL 3 MV Van de Graaff accelerator
Enriched targets of thickness l-3 pg/cm2 were used in
these experiments.
Neutrons from the 54Cr(p,n) reaction were
detected with BF3 counters embedded in a polyethylene
matrix. The assembly subtended approximately 2pi sr at
the target and had an efficiency of about 10%.
Charged particles were detected using surface-barrier
detectors.
OG
The 52Cr targets were prepared by vacuum deposition
of metallic chromium onto carbon backings
approximately 20 mu-g/cm2 thick.
Reduced width = Gc/2Pc
where Gc - the partial width,
Pc - the penetrability in channel c.
No information on sources of uncertainties.
The 50Cr targets were deposited onto backings of
10 mu-g/cm2 of carbon reinforced by a thin layer of
Formvar.
No information on uncertainties

Reduced width for inelastic scattering to the
first-excited state of 52Cr
For inelastic scattering to the first-excited state of
52Cr
Channel spin=1.5
Channel spin=2.5
The elastic and inelastic data were fitted
simultaneously. Absolute cross sections for the
inelastic scattering were determined by comparison
with the elastic data (which were normalized off
resonance to Rutherford scattering). The non-resonant
background in the inelastic scattering was subtracted
before fitting.
No information on sources of uncertainties.
The 54Cr targets were deposited onto backings of
10 mu-g/cm2 of carbon reinforced by a thin layer of
Formvar.
No information on uncertainties

digitized by GSYS2.4.7.
The 54Cr targets were deposited onto backings of

Formvar.
Reduced width = Gc/2Pc
where Gc - the partial width,
Pc - the penetrability in channel c.
Neutron reduced width
No information on sources of uncertainties.
The neutron reduced widths were calculated assuming
that 1.5- resonances decay exclusively to the 3+
ground state of 54Mn and that 0.5+ and 0.5-
resonances decay exclusively to the 2+ first excited
state (55 keV) of 54Mn.
